methods.description: Analyze the statistical relationship between climate variables (temperature, precipitation) and tree-ring growth using the DendroCLIM 2002 software, employing bootstrap resampling for significance testing.

steps.description: the DendroCLIM 2002 programme was applied (Biondi and Waikul 2004).

steps.description: Climate data from October of the previous year to September of the current year served as independent variables

steps.description: the residual chronologies for each site were used as dependent variables.
steps.searchable_summary: Select residual tree-ring chronologies as dependent variables.

steps.description: This program applies a bootstrap procedure to estimate the error using random sampling from the data. DENDROCLIM2002 uses 1,000 bootstrapped samples
steps.searchable_summary: Apply bootstrap resampling (1000 samples) for error estimation and significance testing.

methods.description: Obtain and preprocess climate data (temperature and precipitation) from a meteorological station, including filtering temperature data.

steps.description: Mean monthly temperatures... were collected from the Bydgoszcz meteorological station (from 1861 to 2000)
steps.searchable_summary: Download or retrieve mean monthly temperature data from Bydgoszcz meteorological station for 1861-2000.
outputs: Time series data of mean monthly temperatures

steps.description: monthly precipitation sums were collected from the Bydgoszcz meteorological station (from 1861 to 2000)
steps.searchable_summary: Download or retrieve monthly precipitation sum data from Bydgoszcz meteorological station for 1861-2000.
outputs: Time series data of monthly precipitation sums

steps.description: DplR (Bunn 2008) was used to filter the temperature data.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply filtering to temperature data using the DplR package/methods.

steps.description: A low frequency Hanning filter with a 51-year length weighting was applied, using the formula in dplR according to Oppenheim et al. (1999).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply a low-frequency Hanning filter with a 51-year window to temperature time series data using DplR.
outputs: Filtered temperature time series data (low frequency component)

methods.description: Reconstruct past climate (implied temperature) using a linear regression model based on tree-ring chronologies and actual climate data, perform general statistical calculations (regression values, correlations) using R, and validate the reconstruction by comparing it to actual data and calculating correlations.

steps.description: Statistical calculations such as linear regression values for a regression model and correlations were calculated by means of R (R Development Core Team 2007; Verzani 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate linear regression model parameters and correlation coefficients using R.
outputs: Linear regression model values (coefficients, R-squared, etc.)
